name: Fraga das Penhas Trail
name_original: Trilho do Vale do Rossim à Nave da Mestra — Serra da Estrela
description: Hike the moderate 8.9-mile Fraga das Penhas Trail in Serra da Estrela, featuring the Rossim Valley and Nave da Mestra.
region_intro: Hiking around Mangualde Da Serra offers trails through varied landscapes, including river valleys, forested areas, and granite outcrops characteristic of the region. The terrain often features rolling hills and elevated paths, providing views of the surrounding natural environment. This area is situated within a mountainous region, offering a mix of ascents and descents. Best hiking trails around Mangualde Da Serra The most popular hiking route is Fraga das Penhas Trail, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of forest paths and open terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Covão dos Conchos Trail – Long Lake loop from Vale do Rossim, a difficult 14.1 miles (22.7 km) path. This challenging trail explores high-altitude landscapes and takes approximately 6 hours 27 minutes. Local hikers also love the Vale do Rossim Reservoir loop from Vale do Rossim, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through reservoir surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Hiking around Mangualde Da Serra is defined by river valleys, granite formations, and forested paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Mangualde Da Serra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Mangualde Da Serra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: Mangualde Da Serra offers a diverse network of over 10 hiking trails. These routes vary in difficulty, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging mountain paths, ensuring options for different experience levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: Yes, Mangualde Da Serra has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19431955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir loop from Vale do Rossim&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.0-mile (4.8 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 21 minutes to complete, offering pleasant views around the reservoir.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of hikes in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: Hikes in Mangualde Da Serra vary significantly. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19431955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is around 3 miles (4.8 km) and takes just over an hour. Longer, more challenging trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5304265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail – Long Lake loop from Vale do Rossim&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 14.1 miles (22.7 km) and require over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Mangualde Da Serra are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1270071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Vale do Rossim&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.1-mile (8.1 km) trail.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including river valleys, forested areas, and distinctive granite outcrops. You'll encounter rolling hills and elevated paths that provide scenic views of the natural environment, typical of a mountainous region.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Hikers can discover several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique 'drain,' and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; are popular. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3477370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the hidden &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the region offers spots for wild swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/974890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach&lt;/a&gt; is a known location where you can cool off after a hike. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3477370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; area provides access to water.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Mangualde Da Serra, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to granite formations, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, experienced hikers will find challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5304265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail – Long Lake loop from Vale do Rossim&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.1-mile (22.7 km) path that explores high-altitude landscapes and requires approximately 6 hours 27 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find places to eat or relax near the trails?
A: Near some popular hiking areas, you can find amenities. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2574334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim River Beach Restaurant&lt;/a&gt; is located close to trails around the Vale do Rossim Reservoir, offering a place to eat and relax.

Q: Is parking generally available at trailheads in Mangualde Da Serra?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, areas like Vale do Rossim, a common starting point for several routes, typically offer designated parking facilities for hikers. It's advisable to check specific route information for the most accurate parking guidance.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Mangualde Da Serra suitable for dogs?
A: Many natural areas and trails in Portugal, including those around Mangualde Da Serra, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations or specific trail guidelines, particularly within protected natural parks, to ensure a pleasant experience for both you and your pet.
tips: At over 1,400 meters above sea level, the Rossim Valley Reservoir is a haven of granite, pine trees, and crystal-clear waters in the heart of the Serra da Estrela Mountains. It's the perfect setting for strolls, summer swims, and admiring nature in all its splendor, one of Portugal's highest jewels.

At an altitude of 1,700 metres, in the heart of the Serra da Estrela, stands the impressive Nave da Mestra, a granite colossus that holds a centuries-old secret: the Barca Herminius. Built in 1910 by craftsmen from Manteigas, who climbed the mountain with mules and hydraulic jacks, this house nestled between rocks looks like something out of a fairy tale. It is said that it was designed as a summer retreat by the Matos Preto family, but the legend goes further: it is said that clandestine meetings against the regime, led by judge José Pereira de Matos, took place there. That is why, to this day, it is called the Judge's House.

Very close to Fragão das Penhas Douradas, this spot offers a stunning view over the Rossim Valley Reservoir. In spring, the scenery becomes even more magical: the vibrant hues of the flowers dot the landscape, transforming it into a true natural painting.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Fraga das Penhas Trail is approximately 14.3 km long and typically takes around 4 hours and 10 minutes to complete. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. It involves an elevation gain of about 380 meters, so hikers should be prepared for some uphill sections. While generally accessible, some parts might require a good level of fitness.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery, including stunning views of the Vale do Rossim Reservoir. You'll also pass by the historic Casa do Juiz (Nave da Mestra) and enjoy panoramic vistas from the View of the Vale do Rossim Reservoir. The area is known for its natural beauty within the Serra da Estrela.

faqs.question: Is the Fraga das Penhas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers and livestock. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is usually more stable and pleasant. Winter can bring snow and ice, making conditions challenging, especially at higher elevations in Serra da Estrela.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Fraga das Penhas Trail or to access the Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela. However, always respect park regulations regarding waste, fire, and wildlife.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the Serra da Estrela region, near Vale do Rossim and Nave da Mestra. Specific trailhead access points often have nearby parking areas, especially around popular spots like the Vale do Rossim Reservoir. We recommend checking the komoot map for the most precise starting point and parking options.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers significant sections of the 'Fraga das Penhas' trail itself (78%) and also intersects with parts of the 'Rota do Carvão' (52%) and the 'Grande Rota das Aldeias Históricas - Variante' (20%). You might also encounter smaller sections of trails like 'Senhora do Espinheiro - Sabugueiro - Penhas Douradas' and 'Rota do Maciço Central - Derivação Nave da Mestra'.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, typical of the Serra da Estrela. You can expect a mix of well-defined paths, some rocky sections, and possibly some dirt tracks. Given its location, you'll likely encounter natural features like streams, granite formations, and areas with rich flora.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela is rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot various bird species, including raptors. Mammals like wild boars are present, though often shy. Keep an eye out for diverse insect life, including butterflies and dragonflies, especially near water sources.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for hiking in Parque Natural da Serra da Estrela?
faqs.answer: As part of a Natural Park, hikers should adhere to general regulations: stay on marked paths, do not disturb wildlife or plants, take all trash with you, and be mindful of fire risks, especially during dry seasons. Camping is restricted to designated areas, and drones may require special permission. Always check the official park website for the most current rules.

waypoints.name: View of the Vale do Rossim Reservoir
